findings.description: Replay detection logic in apply_phase_outcome is incomplete. It only compares handoff_id (line 947), omitting the phase check required by Design D4 and the function's own docstring (lines 908-912). This allows a stale handoff_id from a different phase to be incorrectly accepted as a replay, potentially skipping state updates for the current phase.

The discover_agents MCP tool (lines 1040-1066) does not surface the new phase_archetype field in its response dictionary. While the field was added to AgentInfo and the HTTP API surface, the MCP implementation manually builds a dictionary that omits it, breaking observability parity for MCP clients.

The heartbeat MCP tool (line 1070) has not been updated to accept or forward the phase_archetype argument. This prevents agents using the MCP transport from reporting their active archetype via heartbeats, even though the underlying DiscoveryService and HTTP API now support it.

Orphaned resolution cache files may persist indefinitely. In apply_phase_outcome, the function returns early on replay (line 970) without attempting to unlink the cache file. If a previous run crashed after LoopState was persisted but before the cache was unlinked, the file remains on disk even after a successful resume.

In apply_phase_outcome, if the resolution cache file is unparseable or has a checksum mismatch, the function records phase_archetype as None (lines 961, 989). This correctly falls back to legacy behavior but may hide integration bugs where the cache is being malformed by build_phase_dispatch_kwargs.
